name: Bab 00 Office Anomaly Voice
required: Use 'gua' and 'lo' naturally as the core texture.
Use office/tooling artifacts: logs, Slack, Jira, terminal, code, system output, timestamps.
Anomaly must feel small before it feels cosmic.
Show emotion through behavior, silence, code, cursor, logs, and small actions.
Use fragmented rhythm and negative space.
forbidden: Do not write in third-person omniscient style.
Do not refer to GUA as 'dia' as the main narration mode.
Do not invent long prior relationship between GUA and LO ('bertahun-tahun bekerja bersama', 'sudah kenal lama') unless explicitly present in fork record.
Do not explain the system/app to the reader.
Do not turn the scene into generic workplace literary prose.
Do not use 'aku'/'kamu' for GUA/LO — use 'gua'/'lo'.

mood_guardrail.description: Bab 00 humor is dry, corporate, and uncanny — not pop-culture meme comedy.
allowed_humor: Understated irony — the reader notices more than the narrator.
Office rituals made strange (standup, Jira, Slack, ON TRACK).
forbidden_humor: No pop-culture meme references (Shrek, Donkey, Star Wars, Marvel, anime quotes, etc.).
No generic workplace comedy (coffee machine jokes, 'Monday blues', 'IT guy tropes').
No sitcom-style banter between GUA and LO.
No slapstick or exaggerated physical comedy.
No self-aware 'meta' humor where characters know they're in a story.
mood_guardrail.tone_note: If humor arises, it should come from system logs, timestamp anomalies, or the quiet strangeness of two people sharing an error they shouldn't understand. The anomaly is uncanny before it is funny.

style_markers: Fragmented rhythm with deliberate line breaks.
Terminal/code blocks prefixed with > or in monospace blocks.
Corporate language made strange (ON TRACK, alignment, support, collaborated).
Quiet dry observation rather than emotional declaration.
Negative space between paragraphs carries meaning.
System artifacts interrupt narrative flow naturally.
